title: [lora_l2.c:91-109] lora_transition unconditionally forces ABORTED on invalid transition
description:
Gap: Invalid state transitions force ABORTED state even when the caller might handle the error gracefully.

What: Lines 91-109 define lora_transition() which forces ABORTED state on any invalid transition. This is a fail-safe design, but it means programming errors (calling start() twice, etc.) permanently require deinit/init cycle even when the operation could simply fail with an error.

Why it matters: While fail-safe is appropriate for hardware failures, programming errors that cause invalid transitions could be handled more gracefully. For example, calling start() when already running should just return -EALREADY without corrupting state. The current design conflates 'unexpected state' with 'hardware fault'.
close_reason: Intentional fail-safe design: The ABORTED state on invalid transitions is defensive by design (see comment lines 83-84). The state machine treats programming errors as requiring full reinitialization to ensure consistent state. This conflation of 'unexpected state' with 'hardware fault' is intentional - both require operator attention and deinit/init cycle. Graceful handling of double-start() would hide bugs.
